comments.author: @SecretLandscapes4K
comments.text: It’s amazing how these isolated islands have managed to preserve such pristine, untouched nature. 🌿🌍

comments.author: @NovaTerrain-4K
comments.text: Watching the segment where the Cozido stew is buried in the hot volcanic ground of Furnas for 6 hours was absolutely fascinating! Has anyone here actually tried this geothermal-cooked dish? Is the sulfur aroma really as strong as described, or does it add a unique flavor that you can't find anywhere else?

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: We’re so glad you liked it; the Azores truly are a hidden paradise! 🏝️🌋

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Thank you so much, we’re thrilled you enjoyed the journey! 🌊✨

comments.text: Ai is so utterly, utterly boring!

comments.author: @thelanternmile
comments.text: The line that stayed with me is that the volcano never really disappeared, it simply became part of everyday life. That's a beautiful way to understand the Azores. Great documentary!

comments.author: @EpicEarthYTB
comments.text: The Azores are absolutely breathtaking! It's incredible to see how nature has shaped these islands with such dramatic volcanic landscapes and lush greenery.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: We’re so glad you enjoyed the scenery; the volcanic beauty of the islands is truly unique! 🌋✨

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: That thinking face has us curious! 😄 What part of the Azores made you think the most? 🌊🏝️

todos são bem vindos ao nosso pequeno paraíso .

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Thank you for watching, and thank you especially for pointing this out! 🙏 Corvo absolutely deserves attention, especially because of its small population, isolation, and unique way of life. Since you’re originally from the Azores, what do you think outsiders misunderstand most about life on the islands—and which part of Corvo would you most want the world to see?

comments.author: @josephrego2527
comments.text: The Azores like the rest of Portugal were meant to be savored, peacefully, quietly and with a joyful heart, pondering the wonder of God's creation.

comments.author: @Viagemem4K
comments.text: Magnifique ! 😍 Les Açores sont un vrai paradis caché 🌿🌊

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Merci beaucoup ! 😍🌿🌊 Les Açores ont vraiment quelque chose de magique. Parmi les paysages de la vidéo, lequel vous a le plus donné envie d’y aller : les lacs volcaniques, les falaises ou les petits villages isolés ?

comments.author: @trueunrealglobe
comments.text: I've watched plenty of travel videos, but this one actually taught me things I didn't know. That's becoming surprisingly rare on YouTube these days.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: That truly means a lot to us. 🙏 We always hope to go beyond beautiful scenery and share something people may not have known before. What surprised you most about the Azores?

comments.author: @ewsieczka6213
comments.text: I was there in 1997. Wonderful place and people.

replies.author: @trueunrealglobe
replies.text: The drone shots were absolutely worth watching in 4K. Some of those landscapes almost looked too unreal to actually exist on Earth.

comments.author: @TruePlanetOffical
comments.text: I honestly had no idea the Azores were this incredible. The way the islands were shaped by volcanoes, yet people turned that harsh landscape into something so peaceful and beautiful, is amazing.
